# Gitlab Read

## Overview

gSage AI — GitLab read-only operations.

## Tool

| Tool | Summary | Permissions | Requires approval | Core |
| --- | --- | --- | :---: | :---: |
| `gitlab_read` | Read GitLab projects, issues, users, branches, commits, milestones and merge requests. No human approval required. | `gitlab:read` | — | — |

## Configuration

> The JSON below goes into the **configtool model** in the web UI (per organisation; can also be scoped to a department).

> **Sensitive fields** (api keys / passwords / tokens) are *omitted* from the JSON below — set them via environment variables (see the _Environment variables_ table) or directly in the encrypted DB row from the web UI.

### Empty configtool template (required fields only)

```json
{
  "url": "",
  "token": ""
}
```

### Example configtool model

```json
{
  "default_per_page": 20,
  "proxy": "",
  "timeout": 30,
  "token": "",
  "url": "",
  "verify_ssl": true
}
```

### Field reference

| Field | Type | Required | Sensitive | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | :---: | :---: | --- | --- |
| `default_per_page` | `integer` | — | — | `20` | Default page size for paginated requests (default: 20). |
| `proxy` | `string` | — | — | `""` | Optional HTTP/HTTPS proxy URL. |
| `timeout` | `integer` | — | — | `30` | HTTP request timeout in seconds (default: 30). |
| `token` | `string` | ✓ | — | `""` | Personal Access Token (PAT).  Needs 'read_api' scope for gitlab_read and 'api' scope for gitlab_manage. |
| `url` | `string` | ✓ | — | `""` | Base URL of the GitLab instance, e.g. 'https://gitlab.com' or 'https://git.yourcompany.com'.  Must NOT include /api/v4. |
| `verify_ssl` | `boolean` | — | — | `true` | Verify TLS certificate of the GitLab server (default: true). |

## Environment variables

The variables below are derived automatically from each tool's `config_schema`. Set them in your `.env` (or via your secrets backend) to seed defaults for every organisation. **Per-org DB config takes precedence over env vars.**

| Variable | Maps to field | Type | Default | Sensitive | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| `TOOL_GITLAB_READ__DEFAULT_PER_PAGE` | `default_per_page` | `integer` | `20` | — | Default page size for paginated requests (default: 20). |
| `TOOL_GITLAB_READ__PROXY` | `proxy` | `string` | `""` | — | Optional HTTP/HTTPS proxy URL. |
| `TOOL_GITLAB_READ__TIMEOUT` | `timeout` | `integer` | `30` | — | HTTP request timeout in seconds (default: 30). |
| `TOOL_GITLAB_READ__TOKEN` | `token` | `string` | `""` | — | Personal Access Token (PAT).  Needs 'read_api' scope for gitlab_read and 'api' scope for gitlab_manage. |
| `TOOL_GITLAB_READ__URL` | `url` | `string` | `""` | — | Base URL of the GitLab instance, e.g. 'https://gitlab.com' or 'https://git.yourcompany.com'.  Must NOT include /api/v4. |
| `TOOL_GITLAB_READ__VERIFY_SSL` | `verify_ssl` | `boolean` | `true` | — | Verify TLS certificate of the GitLab server (default: true). |

## Operations

### `gitlab_read`

- **`get_issue`** — _(no description)_

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `issue_iid` | `integer` | — | [get_issue, list_issue_notes] Issue IID (internal to project). |

- **`get_project`** — _(no description)_

  | Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
  | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
  | `profile` | `string` | — | GSageToolConfig profile to use.  Omit to use the 'default' profile (most common).  Useful when the org has multiple GitLab instances configured. |
  | `project` | `string` | — | [Most actions] Project identifier: numeric ID (e.g. '42') or namespace/path (e.g. 'mygroup/myrepo'). |
  | `user_id` | `integer` | — | [get_user] GitLab user ID. |
  | `issue_iid` | `integer` | — | [get_issue, list_issue_notes] Issue IID (internal to project). |
  | `max_results` | `integer` | — | Maximum number of items to return (hard cap 500). Default: 20. |
  | `search` | `string` | — | [list_projects, list_users, list_branches, list_labels, list_milestones, list_project_members] Free-text search string. |
  | `visibility` | `string` | — | [list_projects] Filter by visibility. |
  | `membership` | `boolean` | — | [list_projects] When true, list only projects where the token owner is a member. |
  | `archived` | `boolean` | — | [list_projects] Include/exclude archived projects. |
  | `state` | `string` | — | [list_issues, list_merge_requests, list_milestones] Filter by state. |
  | `labels` | `array` | — | [list_issues, list_merge_requests] Filter by label names (all labels must match — AND semantics). |
  | `assignee` | `string` | — | [list_issues, list_merge_requests] Filter by assignee username (e.g. 'joao.silva').  Use the GitLab username, not the display name. |
  | `author` | `string` | — | [list_issues, list_merge_requests] Filter by author username. |
  | `milestone` | `string` | — | [list_issues, list_merge_requests] Filter by milestone title. Use '%23upcoming' for the next open milestone or '%23started' for started milestones. |
  | `due_before` | `string` | — | [list_issues] Issues due before this date (ISO 8601, e.g. '2026-05-31'). |
  | `due_after` | `string` | — | [list_issues] Issues due after this date (ISO 8601). |
  | `scope` | `string` | — | [list_issues] Scope of issues to return. |
  | `issue_search` | `string` | — | [list_issues] Full-text search in issue title and description. |
  | `sort` | `string` | — | [list_issues, list_merge_requests] Sort direction. |
  | `order_by` | `string` | — | [list_issues] Order by field: created_at, updated_at, priority, due_date, relative_position, label_priority, milestone_due, popularity, weight. |
  | `ref` | `string` | — | [list_commits] Branch, tag or commit SHA to list commits for (defaults to project default branch). |
  | `since` | `string` | — | [list_commits] Only commits after this datetime (ISO 8601, e.g. '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z'). |
  | `until` | `string` | — | [list_commits] Only commits before this datetime (ISO 8601). |
  | `referenced_issue_iid` | `integer` | — | [list_commits] When set, only return commits whose message contains a reference to issue #<iid>  (e.g. 'Closes #42', 'Refs #42').  Filtered client-side after fetching. |
